cells.value: +FPDS obligations - SCN / Navy other / AF / Army / DW other
OMN 1B2B - Ship Operational Support & Training (SAG total)
Pre-planning, engineering, submarine + carrier operational support. Source: FY2026 President's Budget OMN Book line 620.
All Navy ship depot maintenance O&M,N customer budget. Source: OMN Book line 6714.
OMN 1B5B - Ship Depot Operations Support (SAG total)
Depot labor, facilities, LCS single-crew support, SUPSHIP. Source: OMN Book line 622.
Start of the waterfall. Deliberately excludes 1B1B Mission/Other Ship Ops (~$7.3B) which funds fleet ops + crew training, not MRO.
§3 - Top-down: CE 928 by-contract slice across the three SAGs  [BAR 3]
OMN 1B2B - CE 928 Ship Maintenance By Contract
Private-contractor slice within 1B2B. Source: OMN Book line 5255.
OMN 1B4B - CE 928 Ship Maintenance By Contract
Primary MRO contract bucket. Source: OMN Book line 6698.
OMN 1B5B - CE 928 Ship Maintenance By Contract
Private-contractor slice within 1B5B. Source: OMN Book line 7217.
OMN Contracted Slice (CE 928 total across 3 SAGs)
Intermediate end-bar. Navy's self-reported 'by contract' share of ship-maintenance SAG spending (top-down accounting, not FPDS-derived).
Decrement in the waterfall. Top-down residual: OMN ship-maintenance authority minus the CE 928 contracted slice.
of which: Public naval shipyard NWCF labor (est.)
Deck evidence anchor, ~$7.5B. Largest labor sub-component; funded via NWCF cross-charges from OMN customers.
of which: Material, IMA, government support (implied)
Material & consumables, intermediate-level maintenance (IMA/SIMA), planning, quality, scheduling, and other non-contract government activities.
§5 - Bottom-up: FPDS MRO obligations by appropriation (TAS)

TAS rollup is the FPDS bottom-up view. Pre-rebase totals to ~$7.36B; post-rebase scales each appropriation's share to the $7,067M Services MRO TAM (post-shore-base-exclusion) via the factor NAVY_TAM_SVC+CG_TAM_SVC over MRO_TAS_TOTAL_FY25 / 1000. The rebase preserves per-appropriation ratios exactly.
Pre-exclusion MRO-PSC universe measured via TAS attribution.
TAS total (post-rebase, should equal NAVY_TAM_SVC+CG_TAM_SVC = Services TAM)
Proves the rebase closes to Services MRO TAM $7,067M.

Expected ~0.954. The ~4.6% gap is the shore-base / FMS / inactive-vessel PIID-level exclusions that services.py strips from the post-exclusion Services TAM ($7,067M) but the USAspending TAS rollup ($7,406M pre-exclusion) includes. Rebasing assumes exclusions distribute proportionally across appropriations.
Addback bars are FPDS MRO obligations funded from appropriations OTHER than OMN. Uses post-rebase values so the sum lands cleanly at the Services MRO TAM (bar 8) net of the OMN slice.
Modernization procurement; includes BA-7 equipment installs, BA-8 spares, BA-1 ships support.
Coast Guard cutter sustainment + ISVS + routine OE depot work.
Strategic Systems Programs (Draper MK7 Trident sustainment); other SMDC/SSP work.
+FPDS obligations - SCN / Navy other / AF / Army / DW other / Other agency
Sum of 6 minor appropriations * rebase factor
SCN PDA/PSA warranty work, APN/WPN/MPN, cross-service flows, non-DoD agency flows.
Should equal Services MRO TAM minus OMN post-rebase slice (i.e., 7,067 - rebased-OMN).
Independent computation of the cross-appropriation residual. Should match 6.T to within $1M rounding.
Navy services-PSC MRO TAM (68 J/K/N/M/H/L codes, post-exclusion)
FY25 FPDS obligations on Navy side, filtered to MRO_PSCS, shore-base excluded.
FY25 FPDS obligations on USCG side, filtered to MRO_PSCS, shore-base excluded.
Intermediate end-bar. Headline services-PSC TAM before PSC 1905 addback.
Classifier output from workbook_build.psc_1905_classifier across 201 PSC 1905 PIIDs. 6-tier cascade (TAS-confirmed + strong keyword + POP + hull). Bounds 1,553 / 1,904 / 2,057.
EB SSN Seam Split Repairs ($831M) + USS Boise EOH ($424M) + other submarine-hull embedded MRO.
CVN-68 class inactivation + CVN-69 PIA + RCOH support.
DDG 1000 BYMP; DDG modernization; cross-class support.
Labor support / SUPSHIP general / cross-class mods not attributable to a specific supergroup.
Reconciled FPDS-visible MRO TAM (Services + PSC 1905 embedded)
End of the waterfall. Replaces the prior $7,067M services-only headline deck-wide.

Paste-ready for think-cell. Row 1 = bar labels (column headers). Row 2 = values: s/e bars carry the running total, d bars carry the negative delta. Row 3 = bar-type codes (reference only - apply in think-cell after paste). The paste block is wider than the rest of the sheet; columns A..K used. Copy A-to-K for rows 1 and 2 into think-cell; mark bar types per row 3.
Companion view to the waterfall. Each cell is a single measurement basis (no mixed obligations + authority within a cell). Highlights where the reconciled $8,971M sits in the broader scope space.

FPDS-visible MRO cell ($8,971M) is the deck headline. FPDS-visible Adjacent cell shows PSC 1905 net newbuild ($38,053M gross less $1,904M embedded MRO); does NOT include PSC 4470 reactor procurement or other newbuild-class PSCs (not currently wired as named ranges).

Not-FPDS MRO cell ($7,500M) is public naval shipyard NWCF labor estimate from PUBLIC_SHIPYARD_NWCF anchor; funded through OMN cross-charges, not via private contract (hence not addressable via FPDS but critical for 'total Navy MRO activity' context).
Waterfall closure: BAR 3 + BARS 4-7 + BAR 9 should equal BAR 10
Expected 8,971. Small mismatch OK - arises from the CE 928 / MRO_TAS_OMN parity gap (see 12.3).
Services TAM closure: CE 928 + addbacks (4-7) vs Services MRO TAM
The gap between this sum and $7,067M IS the CE 928 / MRO_TAS_OMN parity gap. Expected |<$300M|.
CE 928 / MRO_TAS_OMN parity gap (top-down vs bottom-up OMN)
Positive = Navy accounting reports more contracted $ than USAspending maps to OMN on MRO PSCs. Negative = opposite. Expected |<$300M| per methodology doc.
Public-yard anchor vs non-contract residual (PUBLIC_SHIPYARD_NWCF <= BAR 2 residual?)
Positive = residual is large enough to contain public-yard labor plus material/IMA/support. Should be ~$6B.
Structural check on the Reconciliation deck anchor formula.
OP-5 Table IV total should equal OMN 1B4B SAG total

Cross-check: the OP-5 avail-category decomposition (op5_navy_top_down sheet) must sum to the same $11.76B as the existing 1B4B SAG row on Reconciliation. Any non-zero here = data error in navy_op5_1b4b.yaml.
Sum of sourced top-down: OP-5 + MSC + SCN + USCG. Full top-down with OPN/WPN $500M each plan estimates reaches ~$16B (see TAM Bridge sheet).
Public NSY structural gap (dominant gap component)
Public NSY federal civilian labor is invisible to FPDS by construction. This IS the dominant gap (~$7.5B) and is structural, not methodological.
See Verification Answers sheet Section 6.5 for the FPDS-side comparison. Current result: gap ~18% (CLOSE; timing + FDNF scope drivers).
USCG has no OP-5 Table IV equivalent. $120M ISVS is the source-pinned floor. Actual USCG cutter MRO likely $300M-$500M; see MSC SCN USCG Top-Down sheet.
